
### out-of-order and hazards  {.smaller}


* out-of-order execution makes hazards harder to handle 

<hr class="vspace" />

* problems for forwarding: 
   * [value in last stage may not be most up-to-date]{.fragment fragment-index=3 .custom .myem-only}
   * older value may be written back before newer value?
* problems for branch prediction: 
   * mispredicted instructions may complete execution before squashing
* which instructions to dispatch? 
   * how to quickly find instructions that are ready?


### read-after-write examples (1)  {.smaller}

![](/ooo/texfig/rawExamples1.figure.svg){width=800px}

<hr />
 
::: {.myoverbox .fade-in-then-out .fragment fragment-index=1 .vlow}

normal pipeline: two options for %r8? <br> choose the one from <i>earliest stage</i> <br> because it's from the most recent instruction 

:::
 
<div class="fragment fade-in" data-fragment-index=2 >

![](/ooo/texfig/rawExamples1_2.figure.svg){width=900px}
 
</div>

::: {.myoverbox .high fragment-index=3 .fragment .fade-in-then-out}

out-of-order execution:<br/>
`%r8` from earliest stage might be from delayed instruction<br/>
can't use same forwarding logic

:::


### register version tracking  {.smaller}


* goal: track <em>different versions of registers</em>
* out-of-order execution: may compute versions at different times
* only forward the <em>correct version</em> 
<hr class="vspace" />
* strategy for doing this: preprocess instructions represent version info
* makes forwarding, etc. lookup easier


### rewriting hazard examples (1)  {.smaller}

<table class="collines">
<tr><td><tt>addq %r10, %r8</tt></td><td><tt>addq %r10<sub>v1</sub>, %r8<sub>v1</sub> $\rightarrow$ %r8<sub>v2</sub></tt></td></tr>
<tr><td><tt>addq %r11, %r8</tt></td><td><tt>addq %r11<sub>v1</sub>, %r8<sub>v2</sub> $\rightarrow$ %r8<sub>v3</sub></tt></td></tr>
<tr><td><tt>addq %r12, %r8</tt></td><td><tt>addq %r12<sub>v1</sub>, %r8<sub>v3</sub> $\rightarrow$ %r8<sub>v4</sub></tt></td></tr>
</table>
 <hr />
 

* read different version than the one written 

   * represent with three argument psuedo-instructions

* forwarding a value? must match version <i>exactly</i> 
<hr class="vspace" />
* for now: version numbers
* later: something simpler to implement


### write-after-write example  {.smaller}

<!-- \lstset{
    language=myasm,
    morekeywords={mrmovq,irmovq,rmmovq,rrmovq,addq},
    moredelim=**[is][\btHL<2|handout:0>]{~2~}{~end~},
    moredelim=**[is][\btHL<4|handout:0>]{~3~}{~end~},
    moredelim={**[is][\btHL<2,4|handout:0>]{~23~}{~end~}},
} -->
             

::: {.r-stack}
![](/ooo/texfig/wawExamples.figure-1.svg){.fragment .fade-out fragment-index=2 width=900px}

![](/ooo/texfig/wawExamples.figure-2.svg){.fragment .fade-in-then-out fragment-index=2 width=900px}

![](/ooo/texfig/wawExamples.figure-3.svg){.fragment .fade-in-then-out fragment-index=3 width=900px}

![](/ooo/texfig/wawExamples.figure-4.svg){.fragment .fade-in-then-out fragment-index=4 width=900px}

:::

::: {.myoverbox .vlow .fragment .fade-in-then-out fragment-index=2}

many instructions producing differnt version of `%r8` <br />
most recently run instruction may be writing _outdated version_

:::

::: {.myoverbox .vlow .fragment .fade-in-then-out fragment-index=3}

multiple instructions that haven't started<br/>
could need _different versions_ of `%r8`

:::


### keeping multiple versions  {.smaller}


* for write-after-write problem: need to keep copies of multiple versions 

   * both the new version and the old version needed by delayed instructions

* for read-after-write problem: need to distinguish different versions
* solution: <em>have lots of extra registers</em>
* <em>… and assign each version a new ‘real’ register</em> 
<hr class="vspace" />
* called <em>register renaming</em>


### register renaming 


* rename <i>architectural registers</i> to <i>physical registers</i>
* different physical register for each version of architectural
* track which physical registers are ready
* compare physical register numbers to do forwarding
